articles.name: Why does the AI agent provide articles in a different language from the generative reply?
articles.title: Why does the AI agent provide articles in a different language from the generative reply?
articles.body:
Question

Why does the AI agent provide articles in a different language from the generative reply?

Answer

By default, the Web Widget detects the language that the customer's browser sets. You can also set a locale to override the browser language. The AI agent replies based on the language of the customer's message. If the message language differs from the detected locale, the AI agent replies in that language, but the articles beneath appear in the locale that the Web Widget detected.

The AI agent matches the message language, and the related articles follow the Web Widget or browser locale. This keeps content consistent with the interface settings.

For more information, see these articles:

  • How does Zendesk set a language for a user in Support?
  • Creating an AI agent to automatically resolve customer issues
THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

Why does the AI agent or chatbot provide help articles in a different language from the generative reply? Why does the answer bot suggest knowledge base articles in another language? Why do suggested articles mismatch the chatbot reply language?

The AI agent or chatbot replies based on the language of the message from the customer or end user. The related help articles follow the Web Widget or browser locale to keep content consistent with the interface settings. If the message language differs from the detected locale, the generative reply uses the message language while the articles beneath use the detected locale.

Can you override the browser language in the Web Widget? Does the chatbot always use the browser locale for help articles?

By default, the Web Widget detects the language from the browser of the end user or visitor. You can set a specific locale to override the browser language. The AI agent matches the message language for generative replies, but the recommended knowledge base articles follow the detected Web Widget or browser locale.

articles.name: Can I define which customers interact with email AI agents?
articles.title: Can I define which customers interact with email AI agents?
articles.body:
Question

I want to provide higher priority to some of my customers and ensure only human agents address their tickets. Can I define which customers interact with email AI agents?

Answer

Yes. You can use user tags and organization tags to exclude customers from interacting with email advanced AI agents for both Essential AI agents and Advanced AI agents.

Note: If you are part of the Custom Agents EAP, you can also create and designate a custom agent to take action on only customers in your account based on action flows that you define around user or organization tags present in the ticket.

Define which customers interact with email Advanced AI agents

Exclude all customers from an organization

If the customers you want to prioritize and exclusively support with human agents are part of an organization, you can use the organization tag escalated_by_ultimate to restrict their access to email advanced AI agents. You must add this tag to the customers' default organization. New tickets from all users in the organization receive the escalated_by_ultimate tag, which cause the advanced AI agents to skip these tickets.

Exclude single customers

To exclude individual customers, add the tag escalated_by_ultimate to the user's profile. Every ticket from this user will have  include this tag, which the advanced AI agents use to skip the ticket.

Define what customers interact with email Essentials AI agents

When you connect the Essentials AI agent with your email channel, Zendesk creates a trigger named Generative reply for <AI Agent’s name>. This trigger automatically sends generative replies to new tickets.

To define what customers will bypass the AI Agent, add a condition to this trigger that excludes tickets with a unique tag, such as no_ai_reply.

Exclude all customers from an organization

If your customers are part of an organization, use the organization tag no_ai_reply to let them skip the interaction with the AI agent. You must add the tag to the customers' default organization. New tickets from all users in this organization will receive the no_ai_reply tag, which the Essentials AI agents use to identify which tickets to skip.

Exclude single customers

To exclude individual customers, add the tag no_ai_reply to the profile of the user. As a result, every ticket from this user will automatically have this tag, which the essential AI agents use to identify and skip these tickets.

For more information, see Adding tags to users and organizations.

THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

Can I define which customers interact with email AI agents? Can I route specific end users to human agents only? Can I exclude certain companies from chatbot responses? Can I restrict customer accounts from advanced AI agent interactions?

Yes. You can use user tags and organization tags to exclude customers or end users from interacting with email advanced AI agents for both Essential AI agents and Advanced AI agents.

How do I exclude an entire organization or customer account from email Advanced AI agents? How do I route all tickets from a company away from advanced chatbots to support persons?

  1. Navigate to the default organization profile of the company you want to prioritize.
  2. Add the organization tag escalated_by_ultimate to this profile.

New tickets from all users in the organization receive this tag, which causes the advanced AI agents to skip these tickets.

How do I exclude a single customer or end user from email Advanced AI agents? How do I stop advanced chatbots from answering an individual requester?

  1. Open the specific user profile of the customer.
  2. Add the tag escalated_by_ultimate directly to the user's profile.

Every ticket from this user includes this tag, which the advanced AI agents use to skip the ticket.

How do I define what customers interact with email Essentials AI agents? How do I bypass the auto-reply or notification rule for specific requesters?

  1. Locate the trigger named Generative reply for <AI Agent's name> that Zendesk creates when you connect the Essentials AI agent with your email channel.
  2. Add a condition to this trigger that excludes tickets with a unique tag, such as no_ai_reply.

This configuration ensures that tickets matching the exclusion tag bypass the generative replies automated action.

How do I exclude an entire organization from email Essentials AI agents? How do I stop the Essentials chatbot from auto-replying to a company account?

  1. Go to the default organization profile for the company.
  2. Add the organization tag no_ai_reply to the profile.

New tickets from all users in this organization receive the tag, which the Essentials AI agents use to identify which tickets to skip.

How do I exclude a single customer from email Essentials AI agents? How do I block the Essentials answer bot from responding to an individual end user?

  1. Open the user profile of the individual customer.
  2. Add the tag no_ai_reply to the profile of the user.

Every ticket from this user automatically carries this tag, which the essential AI agents use to identify and skip these tickets.

What features do I get if I am part of the Custom Agents EAP? Can I create custom action flows for user or organization tags in the EAP?

If you participate in the Custom Agents EAP, you can create and designate a custom agent to take action on only customers in your account based on action flows that you define around user or organization tags present in the ticket.

articles.name: Why can't I create reports or dashboards in Zendesk Explore?
articles.title: Why can't I create reports or dashboards in Zendesk Explore?
articles.body:
Question

Why can't I create reports or dashboards in Explore?

Answer

You might not be able to create reports or dashboards due to plan limitations or permission issues.

Plan limitations

Zendesk Explore offers different plans with different features. Explore Lite is available with standalone Zendesk Support plans, such as Support Team, Support Professional, and Support Enterprise. Explore Lite agents can only access prebuilt dashboards created by Zendesk. Explore Lite plans don't permit users to create custom reports or dashboards.

If your current Explore plan is Explore Lite and you need advanced capabilities, consider an upgrade to your Explore plan.

See Check your plan type or add-ons.

Permission issues

If you have the right subscription plan and still can't create custom reports and dashboards, it might be a user permission issue.

An admin can confirm if your user role includes the necessary permissions to build reports and dashboards.

See Give users access to Explore.

THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

Why can't I create reports or dashboards in Explore? Can I build custom reporting or analytics dashboards? Why is the option to make custom reports unavailable in my help desk?

You cannot create custom reports or dashboards if you have subscription plan limitations or user permission issues. Explore Lite plans do not permit you to create custom reports or dashboards. If you have the correct pricing tier but still cannot build reports, your user role might lack the necessary permissions.

What features does Explore Lite offer for reporting and analytics? Can I customize dashboards on an Explore Lite pricing tier? What access do agents have on the Lite suite version?

Explore Lite provides access only to prebuilt dashboards created by Zendesk. Explore Lite stands as the service level available with standalone Support plans, including Support Team, Support Professional, and Support Enterprise. This plan type does not let you build custom metrics, reports, or dashboards.

articles.name: Can I use AI agents to respond to Telegram messages?
articles.title: Can I use AI agents to respond to Telegram messages?
articles.body:
Question

I need to link my AI agents to the ticket channel so they reply to tickets created from client messages through Telegram. My AI agent does not react to those customer tickets.

Can I make my AI agent reply to messages I receive through Telegram?

Answer

No, there isn't a built-in Telegram integration in Zendesk. Any connection between Telegram and Zendesk, and any AI agent replies, rely on third-party Marketplace apps, such as Telegramer or Oapps.

These apps bring Telegram messages into Zendesk as tickets, but AI agent replies depend on how the app routes messages. This is a limitation of the Telegram app itself. Zendesk does not control the code quality, support the app, or troubleshoot third-party apps.

To fix this issue, verify the app documentation or contact the app provider for troubleshooting steps. Zendesk does not control how these third-party apps handle AI agent replies.

To verify which app is installed on your account, see Manage your installed apps.

Disclaimer: This article provides instructions only. Zendesk does not support third-party apps. If an app exists, contact the app developer at the support email address under App details in the App Marketplace. You can also search for a solution online.

articles.name: Configuring automatic call acceptance for agents in Contact Center
articles.title: Configuring automatic call acceptance for agents in Contact Center
articles.body:
Admins can configure Contact Center so that inbound calls are automatically accepted on behalf of agents. This setting is controlled on a per-agent basis in Amazon Connect Customer, so you can enable auto-accept for some agents and leave it disabled for others.

What's my plan?
Add-on Contact Center

Summary: ◀▼

You can turn on automatic call acceptance for inbound voice contacts so selected agents’ calls are accepted for them. This per-agent setting helps agents handle calls with less clicking, while still letting you enable it only for the users you want. You can also update auto-accept later with bulk edit after users are created.

Admins can configure Contact Center so that inbound calls are automatically accepted on behalf of agents. This setting is controlled on a per-agent basis in Amazon Connect Customer, so you can enable auto-accept for some agents and leave it disabled for others.

When an incoming voice contact reaches an available agent with auto-accept enabled, the contact console may briefly display the call notification before the call is automatically accepted. The call console displays only an Accept option and Show details for any call information (for example, contact attributes). For more information, see Automatically accepting calls.

You can’t configure per-channel auto-accept during user creation when importing users with a .csv template. Instead, create the users first, then use Bulk Edit to update their per-channel auto-accept settings.

To configure calls auto-accept

  1. Access your Amazon Connect instance, see Accessing Amazon Connect and AWS Console from Contact Center.
  2. Under Users, click Users.
  3. In the list of users, select one or more agents, then click Edit.
  4. Under Settings > Contact handling click the Auto-accept contacts toggle to turn automatic acceptance for Voice on.
  5. Click Save.

articles.name: Service Incident - July 9, 2026 - Support | All Pods - Side Conversation PDF Attachments Failing to Load
articles.title: Service Incident - July 9, 2026 - Support | All Pods - Side Conversation PDF Attachments Failing to Load
articles.body:
Summary

On July 9, 2026, from 11:59 UTC to 16:49 UTC, some customers were unable to open PDF attachments from Side Conversations in Agent Workspace, and saw the error message “Failed to load PDF attachment.”

Timeline


July 09, 2026 12:13 PM 
Between 11:59 - 16:49 UTC on July 9th some customers experienced issues accessing attachments on side conversations, resulting in the error message “Failed to load PDF attachment”. We have since reverted the change and attachments are now accessible as expected. We apologize for the disruption this caused.

Customer impact

  • Who was affected: Some Support customers using Side Conversations in Agent Workspace (across multiple pods).
  • What happened: The PDF viewer could spin and then fail with “Failed to load PDF attachment.”
  • When: Approximately 11:59 UTC to 16:49 UTC on July 9, 2026.
  • What was not impacted: Other (non-PDF) attachments continued to work as expected.
  • Data loss / exposure: None.

Root cause

A change deployed to our Side Conversations attachment handling introduced an unintended behavior change for PDF downloads.

Specifically, part of the update that was intended to be controlled behind a feature flag was not fully gated, so the change affected more traffic than intended. That caused the PDF download/streaming flow to break in production, resulting in PDFs failing to load in the Agent Workspace viewer.

Mitigation and recovery

We reverted the change at 16:49 UTC, which restored normal PDF attachment loading.

What we’re doing to prevent this in the future

  • Ensure the full PDF-handling change is completely gated behind the feature flag (so behavior changes are atomic and can’t partially apply).
  • Add automated test coverage to validate both “flag on” and “flag off” behavior.
  • Strengthen pre-production validation to test the full end-to-end behavior in staging for both flag states.
  • Improve monitoring/alerting on the attachment download path so regressions are detected faster.

FOR MORE INFORMATION

For current system status information about Zendesk and specific impacts to your account, visit our system status page. If you have additional questions about this incident, contact Zendesk customer support.

articles.name: My AI agent is not recommending articles to users who are authenticated with JWT
articles.title: My AI agent is not recommending articles to users who are authenticated with JWT
articles.body:
Issue symptoms

I noticed that authenticated users are unable to see recommended articles in messaging. However, article recommendations are enabled in the AI agent messaging behavior.

Resolution steps

End users who are authenticated with JWT can only access articles when their user profile in Zendesk is created before their conversation starts with the AI agent.

For this to happen, ensure the end user exists in your Zendesk database before the user interacts with the Web Widget. This creates the external ID used to check the user profile and their access permissions. User profiles without an external ID cannot see article recommendations, even if they are authenticated with JWT.

For more information, see this article: What are the different types of JWT proposed by Zendesk?

This article describes functionality available only to customers who had a drafted or published AI agent as of February 2, 2025. For information about equivalent functionality in the AI agents - Advanced add-on.
 
THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

Why are authenticated users not seeing recommended articles in messaging? Why is my AI agent or chatbot not recommending help articles to JWT authenticated customers? Why are article recommendations missing for verified end users in the Web Widget?

End users who authenticate with JWT can only see recommended articles if their user profile in Zendesk exists before their conversation starts with the AI agent. If you do not create the customer profile in your Zendesk database prior to the interaction, the system cannot verify the external ID used to check profiles and access permissions.

How do I fix missing article recommendations for JWT authenticated users in messaging? How do I ensure authenticated customers see help articles from the Answer bot?

  1. Create the end user profile in your Zendesk database before the customer interacts with the Web Widget.
  2. Ensure this action generates the external ID needed to check the user profile and permissions.

What are the limitations of article recommendations for JWT authenticated users? Which pricing tiers or product setups support AI agent article recommendations for authenticated customers?

User profiles without an external ID cannot see article recommendations, even when authenticated with JWT and when you enable article recommendations in the AI agent messaging behavior. This specific functionality is available only to customers who had a drafted or published AI agent as of February 2, 2025.

articles.name: How can I reconnect Zendesk QA?
articles.title: How can I reconnect Zendesk QA?
articles.body:
Question

Some of my tickets do not import into Zendesk QA. What can I do?

Answer

If tickets do not appear in Zendesk QA, the connection to your help desk likely expired. This usually happens after a password change, an admin change, or a bulk edit in your help desk. Zendesk QA pauses the import of new conversations until you renew the connection.

To access Connections:

  1. Select the Settings icon (your profile photo)
  2. Select the Toggle sidebar icon
  3. Under Account, select Connections
  4. Check whether the connection status is Active or Inactive
  5. If the connection is inactive, select the icon with three dots on the right, then select Renew connection
Note: The Renew connection button is only available for Zendesk QA accounts that did not migrate from Klaus.
 
THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

Why do some tickets not import into Zendesk QA? Why do tickets stop appearing or sync pause? Why are new conversations missing in my QA tool?

Tickets do not import when the connection to your help desk or ticketing system expires. This connection drop usually happens after a password change, an admin or support person change, or a bulk edit in your help desk. Zendesk QA pauses the import of new conversations automatically until you renew the connection.

How do I reconnect Zendesk QA? How do I renew my help desk connection? How do I fix an inactive connection?

You can restore your connection through the settings menu. Follow these steps to reconnect:

  1. Select the Settings icon, which displays as your profile photo.
  2. Select the Toggle sidebar icon.
  3. Under Account, select Connections.
  4. Check whether the connection status displays as Active or Inactive.
  5. If the connection status is inactive, select the icon with three dots on the right side, then select Renew connection.

Can I renew my connection if my account migrated from Klaus? Is the Renew connection button available for migrated accounts?

No. You can only use the Renew connection button if your Zendesk QA account did not migrate from Klaus.

articles.name: Why can't I see the full bot conversation at the start of a messaging ticket?
articles.title: Why can't I see the full bot conversation at the start of a messaging ticket?
articles.body:
Question

When a new messaging ticket arrives, I don't see the full bot conversation the user went through. Why doesn't the ticket include all the bot interactions?

Answer

You can see the initial bot interaction for 72 hours after the user's last message. After 72 hours without activity, the system clears that interaction and restarts it when the user returns to the bot.

If a user follows the flow and stays inactive for more than 72 hours, the next user message resets the conversation and resumes from where the user left off in the flow. If that user transfers to an agent, the system creates a new messaging ticket, and you do not see the earlier bot interaction history from before that inactivity period.

If a user starts a bot conversation and remains active in the Web Widget and sends messages within 72 hours, you see the full conversation history in the ticket.

Tip: When a messaging conversation approaches inactivity, Zendesk can send inactivity reminders to the user. Admins can now add tags to those reminder tickets to help track inactive conversations, identify follow-ups, and build reporting workflows around them. For more information, see this article: Sending conversation inactivity reminders to end users

For an entire list of messaging resources, see Web and mobile messaging resources.

THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

Why can't I see the full chatbot conversation history at the start of a messaging ticket? Why does a messaging ticket clear the AI agent interaction history? Why is the chat history missing when an end user transfers to a team member?

You only see the initial chatbot conversation history for 72 hours after the customer sends their last message. After 72 hours of inactivity, the system clears the interaction and resets the history when the customer returns to the AI agent.

Can I see the full chat history if the customer remains active? Does Zendesk preserve the chatbot interaction history when messages occur within 72 hours? Will the messaging ticket show the entire conversation if the end user stays active?

Yes. If the customer starts a chatbot conversation, remains active in the Web Widget, and sends messages within 72 hours, you see the full conversation history in the messaging ticket.

What are the time limits for viewing AI agent conversations in a messaging ticket? What happens to the chat history after 72 hours of inactivity? When does Zendesk reset the conversation history and create a new messaging ticket?

The system enforces a 72-hour inactivity limit. If a customer remains inactive for more than 72 hours and then resumes the flow, the next message resets the conversation. If that customer transfers to a team member, the system creates a new messaging ticket and removes the earlier chatbot interaction history.

How can I track inactive messaging conversations? Can admins add tags to conversation inactivity reminders? How do I build reporting workflows for inactive tickets?

Admins can add tags to conversation inactivity reminders sent to customers. Use these tags to track inactive conversations, identify follow-ups, and build reporting workflows.
